title: Monolithic UPDATE on large table — UNDO exhaustion and long lock
expected_feedback: Standard: Monolithic UPDATE on 20M rows creates several problems: (1) holds row locks on all 20M rows for the entire transaction duration, blocking concurrent DML on overlapping rows; (2) generates a massive UNDO segment, risking ORA-30036 (unable to extend undo segment) if UNDO tablespace is insufficient; (3) a single failure rolls back all 20M rows of work. Use PK-range or ROWID-range batching with COMMIT every 1000–10000 rows. This limits UNDO consumption, releases locks incrementally, and allows resume from the last committed batch on failure. Monitor V$UNDOSTAT during the backfill.
